_comment: Canonical frontmatter map for Phase 1 Step 9. Maps each skill to when_to_use / produces / consumes / dispatch-agent. name/description/allowed-tools are kept from existing frontmatter unless a description_override is provided for trigger-conflict disambiguation.

brainstorm.description_override: Turn a fuzzy idea into a concrete, reviewable spec via structured dialogue. THINK stage entry. Triggers: "brainstorm", "design", "spec this out", "let's think through".
brainstorm.when_to_use: Use at the start of any new feature, project, or design decision. Produces a spec document that is the single input to writing-plans. NOT for product-market validation — use office-hours for that (its design-doc output is for product discovery, not sprint pipeline).

careful.when_to_use: Activate before destructive or irreversible operations: delete, overwrite, force-push, drop tables, rm -rf. Complements TACIT-DENSE (which is knowledge-focused, not blast-radius-focused).

code-review.description_override: Structured P0/P1/P2 code review of WRITTEN CODE (not plans). Three modes: giving / receiving / security. Triggers: "review this code", "ready for PR", "address review", "security audit". NOT for reviewing plans — use plan-review for that.
code-review.when_to_use: Use after code is written to review changes, address review feedback, or audit for security. NOT for reviewing implementation plans before build — that is plan-review's job.

dependency-audit.when_to_use: Use when auditing dependencies for security (npm audit), outdated packages, or license compliance. Triggers: "dependency audit", "npm audit", "security scan", "outdated packages", "license check". Complements code-review (which audits application code, not deps).

document-release.when_to_use: Post-ship only. Use after code has shipped to sync README, CHANGELOG, ARCHITECTURE with the diff. Optional — triggers: "update docs", "sync documentation".

executing-plans.description_override: Execute a WRITTEN PLAN step-by-step with verification gates and wave-based sub-agent dispatch. BUILD stage orchestrator. Triggers: "execute plan", "run the plan", "start executing". NOT for writing new code from scratch — use test-driven-development for that.
executing-plans.when_to_use: Use after plan-review approves a plan. Walks the plan step-by-step with verification gates. Dispatches sub-agents in waves when steps are parallelizable.

finishing-branch.when_to_use: Use when a feature branch is complete and ready to merge: final cleanup, sync with main, squash, and PR creation. NOT for authoring the full release — use release for that.

framework-management.when_to_use: Unified entry for creating, installing, listing, and managing both skills and agents (absorbed agent-management + writing-skills). Use it to scaffold a new skill, install from URL, or list built-ins.

frontend-design.when_to_use: Use when building UI, pages, or components. Guides context gathering → implementation → designer-agent quality gate at 7+/10. Triggers: "frontend design", "build UI", "design page".

git-worktrees.when_to_use: Use when working on multiple branches in parallel. Sets up isolated worktrees so concurrent sub-agents can work without stepping on each other.

harness-engineering.when_to_use: Use to audit or improve the agent harness: context efficiency, tool space, evaluation gates, feedback loops. Triggers on 3-strike agent failure signal or explicit harness audit request.

investigate.when_to_use: Use for open-ended exploration of unfamiliar code: "how does X work", "map this system". Complements systematic-debugging (which requires a known error). NOT for known bugs — use systematic-debugging.

office-hours.description_override: Product discovery and idea validation (startup/builder modes). Outputs a design-doc for product decisions, NOT a sprint spec. Triggers: "office hours", "validate my idea", "help me think through a product".
office-hours.when_to_use: Use for product-market validation, narrow-wedge discovery, side-project design thinking. NOT for sprint-pipeline features — use brainstorm for those.

plan-review.description_override: Multi-lens auto-review of an IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (not code). Strategy → Design (if UI) → Engineering phases. All decisions auto-resolved via 6 Decision Principles. Triggers: "review plan", "autoplan".
plan-review.when_to_use: Use automatically after writing-plans completes. NOT for reviewing code — use code-review for that.

production-readiness.when_to_use: Use before deploying to production. Verifies observability, reliability, operability, security, goal alignment. The gate between "tests pass" and "safe to deploy".

qa.when_to_use: Use during VERIFY stage for test coverage, edge cases, auto-fix. Complements verification (which checks acceptance criteria).

refactoring.description_override: Systematic, behavior-preserving code refactoring with safety gates. Dispatches refactoring-agent. Triggers: "refactor", "clean up code", "reduce tech debt", "extract method", "rename". NOT for reactive PR feedback — use code-review for that.
refactoring.when_to_use: Use for proactive tech-debt reduction or structural code improvement. Pair with test-driven-development (tests-first, one-change-at-a-time).

release.description_override: Combined RELEASE stage (ship + retrospective) in a single artifact. Triggers: "/release", "end sprint", "ship and reflect". Replaces the retired ship skill.
release.when_to_use: Use to close a sprint: version bump, changelog, tag, push, publish, and retrospective — all in one artifact.

self-improvement.when_to_use: Use for process retrospection (three scopes: process / retro / harness). NOT for checking whether a specific task is done — use verification for that.

style-capture.when_to_use: Use on project initialization or when asked to capture style preferences. Output feeds brainstorm, executing-plans, code-review, self-improvement to keep outputs stylistically consistent.

subagent-development.description_override: Default working mode for non-trivial tasks. Decompose into waves of 5-10 parallel sub-agents. Triggers: any multi-file / multi-concern task, "use subagents", "parallel implementation".
subagent-development.when_to_use: Use whenever a task spans multiple files, multiple concerns, or benefits from parallel execution. Complementary to executing-plans (which is sequential-with-waves). NOT for writing tests — use test-driven-development for that.

systematic-debugging.when_to_use: Use for KNOWN errors, test failures, or unexpected behavior. Iron Law: no fix without root-cause investigation. NOT for open exploration — use investigate for that.

test-driven-development.description_override: Globally enforced Red-Green-Refactor for WRITING NEW CODE. Triggers on: "implement", "add feature", "fix bug", any code-writing step. NOT for task decomposition — use subagent-development for that.
test-driven-development.when_to_use: Use whenever new code is being written. Mandatory — this skill is a gate on all code-writing activities.

using-skills.when_to_use: Always active — the meta-skill that teaches the framework. Read first on session start. Not invoked explicitly.

verification.description_override: Structured self-check against acceptance criteria BEFORE claiming DONE. Triggers: "I'm done", "verify this", "finished". NOT for sprint retrospection — use self-improvement for that.
verification.when_to_use: Use immediately before reporting DONE on any substantive task. Writes an evaluation artifact that finishing-branch / release consume.

vibe.description_override: Unified entry point: detect current pipeline stage, launch guided workflow, auto-delegate to next skill. Triggers: "/vibe", "activate framework", "start workflow". NOT a reference document — use workflow for that.
vibe.when_to_use: Use to enter the framework, resume mid-sprint work, or auto-chain through stages. The primary user-facing entry command.

workflow.description_override: Reference document describing the 6-stage pipeline and skill data flow. Triggers: "workflow", "pipeline". NOT an action — use vibe to run the pipeline.
workflow.when_to_use: Use as a lookup when you need to know which skill runs at which stage or what artifact each stage produces. Does not produce artifacts itself.

writing-plans.when_to_use: Use after a spec is approved to turn it into an executable plan. Consumes the spec, produces the plan that plan-review and executing-plans will consume.
